summary: PR 2742 made a small data-matching update for `main/melee/gm/gmmain_lib`, touching `gmmain_lib.c` and `gm/types.h`. The diff changed a file-local zero byte from scalar `u8` to initialized `s8[] = { 0 }` and updated uses to index `[0]`; it also expanded `struct gmm_x0` with explicit tail padding and a `STATIC_ASSERT` for size `0x8518`. The bot report says `.bss` and `.sdata` for `main/melee/gm/gmmain_lib` reached 100% data match.

key_files.role: Adjusted a file-local data declaration and its consumers in `gmMainLib_8015F600` for data matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `static u8 gmMainLib_804D3EE4;` changed to `static s8 gmMainLib_804D3EE4[] = { 0 };`
raw_diff: uses changed from scalar casts to `gmMainLib_804D3EE4[0]`

key_files.role: Adjusted `struct gmm_x0` layout with explicit padding and size assertion.

what_changed.text: `gmMainLib_804D3EE4` changed from an uninitialized scalar `u8` to an initialized `s8` one-element array containing zero.
Consumers in `gmMainLib_8015F600` now read the zero terminator through `gmMainLib_804D3EE4[0]` instead of scalar casts.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: while condition and assignments changed to `gmMainLib_804D3EE4[0]`

what_changed.text: `struct gmm_x0` gained explicit padding from offset `0x6E50` to `0x8518`, replacing the prior size comment.
`struct gmm_x0` now has a compile-time size assertion for `0x8518`.
The automated report recorded two new data matches for `main/melee/gm/gmmain_lib`: `.bss` +34072 bytes and `.sdata` +1 byte, both reaching 100%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment decomp-dev[bot]: matched data +68176 bytes; `.bss` 50.02% to 100.00%; `.sdata` 88.89% to 100.00%

smart_moves.text: Kept the data-matching edit narrowly scoped: only the declaration and direct scalar uses of `gmMainLib_804D3EE4` were changed.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `gmmain_lib.c` has two small hunks affecting one static and three uses

smart_moves.text: Used a `STATIC_ASSERT` after changing struct padding so the intended recovered size is checked by the source.

decomp_lessons.text: For data-section matching, a zero-valued symbol may need its declaration shape and section ownership represented explicitly; in this PR a scalar `u8` became an initialized `s8[] = { 0 }`, and call sites indexed `[0]` to preserve the single-byte value use.
When a recovered structure must account for a larger known data footprint, adding explicit tail padding plus `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...))` documents and guards the layout assumption.

naming_conventions.text: No semantic rename was introduced; existing address-style names such as `gmMainLib_804D3EE4` and placeholder field names in `struct gmm_x0` were preserved.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbol and field names retained except adding `pad_6E50`

naming_conventions.text: The new padding field uses offset-based naming, `pad_6E50`, matching the field's starting offset comment.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Represented a one-byte zero datum as an initialized signed-byte array to affect data placement/typing while still reading it as element zero in code.
Used explicit struct tail padding to align the C type size with the recovered expected size for data matching.
Relied on automated data-match reporting as evidence: bot report listed `.bss` and `.sdata` for `main/melee/gm/gmmain_lib` reaching 100%.

evidence_refs: raw_diff: complete 1692-byte diff for both changed files
changed_files.jsonl: two changed files with line counts
notes: PR body is empty and there are no review comments, so reviewer rationale and author explanation are unavailable.
The bot report supports the data-match outcome, but no build log or objdiff details beyond the summarized data percentages are present in the slice.
Lessons about why the scalar-to-array change matched data are inferred from the diff plus bot report and should be curated conservatively.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use explicit tail padding plus size assert when recovering a struct's data footprint
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2742 changed `struct gmm_x0` by replacing an outdated size comment with `u8 pad_6E50[0x8518 - 0x6E50]` and `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct gmm_x0) == 0x8518)`, alongside a bot report showing `gmmain_lib` `.bss` reached 100%. Candidate lesson: explicit tail padding with a compile-time size check is a useful, reviewable way to document a recovered aggregate size for data matching.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Small data declaration shape can matter for data matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2742 changed `gmMainLib_804D3EE4` from scalar `u8` to initialized `s8[] = { 0 }` and adjusted uses to `[0]`; the bot report showed `gmmain_lib` `.sdata` reached 100%. Candidate lesson: for data-matching work, declaration form, signedness, initialization, and scalar-vs-array shape can affect section matching and should be kept narrow and evidence-backed.

source_update_candidates.title: `gmmain_lib` data matching involved `struct gmm_x0` size `0x8518`
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2742 provides evidence that `src/melee/gm/types.h`'s `struct gmm_x0` was padded to size `0x8518` with `pad_6E50[0x8518 - 0x6E50]`, and `src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.c` data matching then reported `.bss` reaching 100% for `main/melee/gm/gmmain_lib`.
source_update_candidates.reason: May be useful as a path-scoped data-layout hint, but should be curated against current headers before accepting.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize this PR into a rule that all zero byte globals should become arrays; the slice only supports this specific `gmMainLib_804D3EE4` data-match edit.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; no review comments are present.
Do not claim source-level semantic names for `struct gmm_x0` fields or `gmMainLib_804D3EE4`; the PR preserved address-style/placeholder naming.
